an1.51:1.2: And yet it is defiled by adventitious defilements.
an1.51:1.3: The uninstructed worldling does not know this as it truly is.
an1.51:1.4: Therefore I say that for the uninstructed worldling there is no development [*bhāvanā*] of the mind [*citta*]."
an1.52:1.2: And yet it is freed from adventitious defilements.
an1.52:1.3: The instructed noble disciple knows this as it truly is.
an1.52:1.4: Therefore I say that for the instructed noble disciple there is development [*bhāvanā*] of the mind [*citta*]."
an1.53:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu* cultivates a mind [*citta*] of *mettā* even for the duration of a finger-snap,
an1.53:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells without neglecting *jhāna*, who acts in accordance with the Teacher's instruction, who follows his advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.53:1.4: What then of those who cultivate it often?"
an1.54:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu* develops a mind [*citta*] of *mettā* even for the duration of a finger-snap,
an1.54:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells without neglecting *jhāna*, who acts in accordance with the Teacher's instruction, who follows his advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.54:1.4: What then of those who develop it often?"
an1.55:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu* attends to a mind [*citta*] of *mettā* even for the duration of a finger-snap,
an1.55:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells without neglecting *jhāna*, who acts in accordance with the Teacher's instruction, who follows his advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.55:1.4: What then of those who attend to it often?"
an1.56:1.1: "Whatever *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, are unwholesome, partaking of the unwholesome, on the side of the unwholesome — all of these are preceded by mind [*citta*].
an1.56:1.2: Mind [*citta*] arises first among those *dhammas*, and unwholesome *dhammas* follow right after."
an1.57:1.1: "Whatever *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, are wholesome, partaking of the wholesome, on the side of the wholesome — all of these are preceded by mind [*citta*].
an1.57:1.2: Mind [*citta*] arises first among those *dhammas*, and wholesome *dhammas* follow right after."
an1.58:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single other thing through which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline, as with heedlessness, *bhikkhus*.
an1.58:1.2: In the heedless one, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.59:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single other thing through which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline, as with heedfulness, *bhikkhus*.
an1.59:1.2: In the heedful one, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.60:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single other thing through which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline, as with laziness, *bhikkhus*.
an1.60:1.2: In the lazy one, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
